lines.en_lit: Rather, It is derived “sheikhar” “sheikhar” of nazirite Just as there, wine, so too, here, wine

Is tirosh wine? Isn’t it taught One vows from tirosh is not allowed sweet foods, but is allowed wine. And is not wine? But isn’t it written: “Tirosh shall make the young women flourish [yenovev]” Something that comes from tirosh, causes virgins to come forth;
But isn’t it written: “And your vats shall overflow with tirosh” the vats shall overflow with something that comes from tirosh,
But it is written: “Harlotry, and wine, and tirosh take away the heart” Rather, according to everyone, tirosh wine, but vows follow colloquial language.
And why does call wine why does it call it tirosh? “Wine” that it brings lament to the world “Tirosh” that those who indulge in it become poor [rash].

Rav Kahana raised a contradiction: It is written tirash but we read it tirosh. one merits he is made a head [rosh]; one does not merit he is made poor [rash]. This is the same as Rava Rava raised a contradiction: It is written: makes glad [yishamaḥ] we read it yisamaḥ If one merits, makes him happy [same’aḥ]; if one does not merit, makes him confounded [shamem]. This is what Rava said: Wine and good scents make me wise,

From where do we bathing and smearing is called affliction? As it is written “I ate no pleasant bread, neither did meat nor wine enter my mouth, neither did I anoint myself at all” What “I ate no pleasant bread”? Rav Yehuda, son of Rav Shmuel bar Sheilat, said: He did not eat even bread refined wheat;

And from where do we considered affliction? As it is written: “Then he said to me: Fear not, Daniel, for from the first day that you set your heart to understand and to afflict yourself before your God, your words were heard, and I have come due to your words” “For you are greatly loved”

We have found smearing from where do we bathing Rav Zutra, son of Rabbi Toviya, said: The verse states: “And it came into his innards like water, and like oil into his bones” But say drinking It is similar oil. Just as oil from outside so too, water from the outside.

But the tanna took opposite as we learned From where do we derive that smearing is like drinking on Yom Kippur? Although there is no proof of the matter there is an allusion to the matter as it is stated: “And it came into his innards like water, and like oil into his bones” Rather, Rav Ashi said: Bathing is derived from the same verse as it is written: “Neither did I anoint myself at all”

What “And I have come due to your words” This is as it is written: “And there stood before them seventy men of the Elders of the house of Israel, and Jaazaniah, son of Shaphan, standing in the midst of them, each man with his censer in his hand, and a thick cloud of incense went up” “And the form of a hand was put forth, and I was taken by a lock of my head; and a spirit lifted me up between the earth and the heaven, and brought me in the visions of God to Jerusalem, to the door of the gate of the inner court that faces northward where
